figures.caption: Table 3: SCALE reliability and sparse-view controls.

Figure 1: Post-retrieval reliability in open-pool LoRA reuse. Prior methods mainly decide which adapters to retrieve and how to weight them before dense composition. SCALE separates the remaining post-retrieval problem into two bottlenecks: merge interference, where relevant adapters may not be parameter-compatible, and view reliability, where several plausible sparse composition views may disagree. LASRC addresses the first bottleneck with anchor-preserving residual composition, while SCALE analyzes the second through agreement, support-loss proxy selection, and oracle headroom under explicit path cost.
